body.title: [triage] 150 sprint tickets carry NO intention — so under today's ruling they cannot be proven. There is no property to break.
body.intention: I intend that no ticket enters sprint without an intention, because the intention IS the property mutation-red breaks — so a ticket without one cannot be proven, and under 'every ticket has a proof, period' it cannot honestly close either. [intention_date: 2026-07-13]
body.description:
**⚠ THE RED RUN IS NOW RECORDED AND IT IS NOT HYPOTHETICAL. 2026-07-13: 149 sprint tickets kicked back to design as FAIL (Akien: 'those should get sent back to the design step as FAIL for learning from and fixing'). One more (T-gate-timeout-test) cancelled as test junk. See notes/fail-149-tickets-no-intention-20260713.**

**sprint is now 163 tickets, ALL 163 carrying an intention — the invariant is TRUE for the first time. THIS GATE'S JOB IS TO HOLD IT, NOT TO ACHIEVE IT.**

**THE LEARNING: ONE HOLE, NOT 149 OVERSIGHTS.** The triage→sprint transition never checked for an intention. 149 tickets did not each get forgotten — ONE GATE WAS ABSENT and let everything through. Counting instances instead of finding the gate is how you fix this 149 times and still ship the 150th.

**AND THE EXTRACTOR WAS NEVER THE GATE.** It was supposed to soften this by inferring an intention at `add` time, and it has been ~99% broken since it shipped. But EVEN WORKING IT WOULD NOT HAVE FIXED THIS: a machine-extracted intention is a RATCHET, not a SPEC (D-provenance-is-the-safety-property-2026-07-13). Only a DECLARED intention gates proof-on-close. Treating the extractor as the gate was a second, quieter error.

**THE GATE:** the triage→sprint transition REJECTS a missing, empty, or DEGENERATE intention (`TBD` / `unknown` / `n/a` / whitespace). A bare presence-check passes all three — and `unknown` is PRECISELY what a broken extractor writes, so a presence-check would have certified 2,435 crash outputs as intentions.

**Affected files:** devlab/claudecode/cc_queue.py (cmd_setstatus / cmd_propose — the triage→sprint transition), unseen_university/gate_logic.py.

**Scope boundary:** IN — the sprint-entry gate and its degenerate-value rejection. OUT — the close-path proof gate (exists); backfilling intentions (REFUSED BY RULING: that papers over the hole).

**Proof obligation.** The hollow build checks `if not t.get('intention'): reject` and passes a green run. What it could NOT pass: (1) a ticket whose intention is the literal string `unknown` is REJECTED at sprint entry — this is THE fixture, because it is the one a presence-check fails and it is the exact value a broken extractor emits; (2) a ticket whose intention is whitespace-only is REJECTED; (3) the recorded red run (149 tickets, 2026-07-13) precedes the green.

**Completion criteria:** 1. triage→sprint rejects missing/empty/degenerate intentions. 2. The `unknown` fixture passes. 3. sprint holds at 100% intention coverage after the gate ships. 4. Red run recorded before green.

**Falsifier:** if a ticket ever reaches sprint again with a missing/empty/degenerate intention, the gate is decorative and the FAIL was tidied up, not learned from.
